name: Russia
contextBlurb: Russia restricts domestic crypto use while piloting a state CBDC and exploring limited cross-border use of crypto. Under Federal Law No. 259-FZ, crypto and stablecoins are not legal tender and cannot be used for domestic payments; tokenized instruments may be issued as digital financial assets on licensed platforms. The digital ruble is enabled by the 2023 law and is in an expanded pilot as a retail payment rail. In October 2025, the EU’s 19th sanctions package introduced the first EU-wide ban on transactions involving the rouble-pegged A7A5 stablecoin and designated related entities. Russia continues to test controlled international-settlement uses through experimental regimes, but broad retail access to stablecoins remains restricted.

legislation.title: EU bans A7A5 transactions and lists related entities.
Finance Ministry proposes easing thresholds for ‘especially qualified’ investors in crypto ELR.
Bank of Russia outlines three-year Experimental Legal Regime for crypto; participation restricted.

stablecoinMeta.summaryEn: Russia restricts domestic crypto use while piloting a state CBDC and exploring limited cross-border use of crypto. Under Federal Law No. 259-FZ, crypto and stablecoins are not legal tender and cannot be used for domestic payments; tokenized instruments may be issued as digital financial assets on licensed platforms. The digital ruble is enabled by the 2023 law and is in an expanded pilot as a retail payment rail. In October 2025, the EU’s 19th sanctions package introduced the first EU-wide ban on transactions involving the rouble-pegged A7A5 stablecoin and designated related entities. Russia continues to test controlled international-settlement uses through experimental regimes, but broad retail access to stablecoins remains restricted.
stablecoinMeta.classificationNote: Russia regulates tokenized instruments under the Digital Financial Assets (DFA) framework; DFAs are property-like digital rights issued and traded on licensed platforms. Stablecoins are not expressly defined in law. A January 2024 State Duma bill would add “secured stablecoins” to the DFA statute an
